title: Error: OCI runtime error: container_linux.go:380: starting container process caused: error adding seccomp filter rule for syscall bdflush: requested action matches default action of filter
body:
[root@localhost home]# cat /etc/redhat-release 
CentOS Linux release 7.6.1810 (Core) 
[root@localhost home]# podman -v
podman version 3.0.1
[root@localhost home]# docker run -dit --name zk_01 --network  master-network --ip 172.20.0.11 zookeeper
Error: OCI runtime error: container_linux.go:380: starting container process caused: error adding seccomp filter rule for syscall bdflush: requested action matches default action of filter

comments.body:
[root@liaobaikai ~]# cat /etc/redhat-release 
CentOS Linux release 7.8.2003 (Core)
[root@liaobaikai ~]# podman -v
podman version 3.0.1
[root@liaobaikai ~]# docker run -dit --name zk_03 --network  master-network --ip 172.20.0.13 zookeeper
bd622a72e3650c3434cdad235b1f308df4de37da5729674b8bc82379923b227d
You are using docker not podman as the `container_linux` error you are getting is a common docker error. Try restarting docker daemon or even better, use podman :-)

That could easily have been Podman with the Docker alias added - that error
is out of runc, not Docker itself, so it can easily happen with Podman.
Usually points to a too old runc or libseccomp.

On Fri, Jul 23, 2021 at 12:16 Charlie Doern ***@***.***>
wrote:

> Closed #11029 <https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11029>.
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you are subscribed to this thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11029#event-5061908937>, or
> unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCEW4MAH5DD54YZGUV3TZGIULANCNFSM5A4HQ3AQ>
> .
>
@mheon I thought -dit was docker only, I will reopen sorry.
still, container_linux in podman also doesnt have a line 80, so I think this is docker

It’s pointing to a line in the runc codebase, which  AFAIK changed heavily
recently for the 1.0 release - a refactor to split the code into
subpackages. That sounds like a reasonable line number given that.

Speaking of runc versions, I suspect upgrading to the most recent will
resolve this.

On Fri, Jul 23, 2021 at 15:23 Charlie Doern ***@***.***>
wrote:

> still, container_linux also doesnt have a line 80, so I think this is
> docker
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
>
>
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11029#issuecomment-885864979>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCANJUWSSO6WGP5VPI3TZG6SZANCNFSM5A4HQ3AQ>
> .
>

> Speaking of runc versions, I suspect upgrading to the most recent will resolve this.

It won't. The fix is in https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/pull/3109, but originally that comes from the fact that podman started to generate redundant seccomp rules recently. I have explained why that happens and what is the fix in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11005

I think we can fix it in podman, too. 

First, we need https://github.com/containers/common/pull/689 (and cut a v0.38.17 release).
One a release is made, we need to bump containers/common to v0.38.17 in podman v3.2 branch.

https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11005#issuecomment-885277879

OK, this sounds like it needs to be a priority backport. Thanks!

So... This version is relatively new.

Upgrade:
sudo curl -L -o /etc/yum.repos.d/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable.repo https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/CentOS_7/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable.repo
sudo yum -y install podman
Opened https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11031 to summarize my understanding of the issue.
@liaobaikai can you report the version of `containers-common` rpm, as well as the output of `grep defaultErrno /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json`?

@kolyshkin containers-common rpm info:
[root@localhost ~]# grep defaultErrno /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json
        "defaultErrnoRet": 38,
[root@localhost ~]#
@liaobaikai what's the version of containers-common rpm? `rpm -qf /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json`
@liaobaikai it seems that you can work around the bug by removing the whole line containing `defaultErrnoRet` from `/usr/share/containers/seccomp.json`

> @liaobaikai what's the version of containers-common rpm? `rpm -qf /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json`

[root@localhost ~]# rpm -qf /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json
containers-common-1-18.el7.20.1.noarch

> @liaobaikai it seems that you can work around the bug by removing the whole line containing `defaultErrnoRet` from `/usr/share/containers/seccomp.json`

As well as  _sed -i '/defaultErrnoRet/d' /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json_  ?

@kolyshkin When I removed the whole line containing defaultErrnoRet from /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json, got the same error.

[root@localhost ~]# podman run -dit --name zk_01 --network  master-network --ip 172.20.0.11 zookeeper
Error: OCI runtime error: container_linux.go:380: starting container process caused: error adding seccomp filter rule for syscall bdflush: requested action matches default action of filter
[root@localhost ~]# 
[root@localhost ~]# 
[root@localhost ~]# 
[root@localhost ~]# grep defaultErrno /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json
[root@localhost ~]#

It looks like they're packaging a too-old containers.conf, recommend you ask them to update.
actually, with `seccomp-18.el7.21.1.json` i get the error. copying over `seccomp-8.el7.13.1.json` on to the newer package version, it works.
